steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: The passage's evidence is the sixth most commonly used password. This statistic ranks actual passwords by popularity, not by complexity or creativity. The entire example centers on frequency of real user choices.

steps.title: Match the signal
steps.content:
Popular directly answers the blank — the example proves that users gravitate toward the most-used (popular) password options when prioritizing convenience. The other three choices either contradict the point (complex) or don't match the frequency-based evidence (creative, useful).

This matches choice D — D is correct.
